topic: tool/kick_bench.hexa parses outer __RC= only, NOT inner __HEXA_RC=. When dispatch shells through docker → inner runtime fails (rc=3) but outer wrapper exits 0 → per-record JSONL status="fire" misleads. Self-found in ω-cycle live-spawn-smoke (commit 2d40c170 freshness guard fixed outer sentinel honesty but per-record JSONL still mis-reported).
rule: honesty: status="fail-inner-rc" when outer=0 inner!=0; do NOT label such records as "fire"

core_5_op_kernel.1_intake_topic: kick_bench.hexa per-record JSONL emits status="fire" whenever outer rc==0, regardless of inner __HEXA_RC. Sample failing record state/kick/bench/2026-04-26_live-spawn-smoke-2-line-haiku-poetic-devices.jsonl: {wall_time_ms:0, witness:"", status:"fire"} for both backends — outer wrapper exited 0 because inner docker exec returned 3 quietly; the bench layer never inspected __HEXA_RC=3.

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: outer-rc-still-parsed-correctly
2_pre_register_falsifiers.criterion: after the patch, parsing __RC=0\n__HEXA_RC=3 still extracts outer rc=0 (existing behavior preserved); parsing __RC=2 still extracts rc=2
2_pre_register_falsifiers.method: code trace: last_index_of(__RC=) finds the explicit shell echo; substring-up-to-whitespace-or-underscore breaks before any concatenated __HEXA_RC=, isolating the integer; to_int with try/catch

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: inner-hexa-rc-now-parsed
2_pre_register_falsifiers.criterion: captured stdout ending in __HEXA_RC=3 yields hexa_rc=3 in the parser; absence of __HEXA_RC= yields hexa_rc=0 (default)
2_pre_register_falsifiers.method: added last_index_of(__HEXA_RC=) + +10 offset + char-walker bounded by whitespace/underscore; to_int with try/catch fallthrough

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: status-fail-inner-rc-emitted-when-applicable
2_pre_register_falsifiers.criterion: rc==0 AND hexa_rc!=0 → status="fail-inner-rc" in the per-record JSONL; counter NOT incremented; freshness guard untouched in this branch (status!="fire" so neither delta=1 nor stale-witness reassignment fires)
2_pre_register_falsifiers.method: freshness smoke C6 (rc=0 hexa_rc=3 fresh-witness) + C7 (rc=0 hexa_rc=3 empty-witness) — both PASS with status=fail-inner-rc delta=0

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: freshness-smoke-regression-zero
2_pre_register_falsifiers.criterion: tool/kick_bench_freshness_smoke.hexa cases C1..C5 (legacy 5) still PASS verbatim; new C6/C7 PASS too; total 7/7

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: dry-run-regression-zero
2_pre_register_falsifiers.criterion: hexa run tool/kick_bench.hexa <topic> --dry-run emits __KICK_BENCH__ PASS backends=N witnesses=0 with one __KICK_BENCH_DRY__ per backend; no parse error from the new __HEXA_RC= block (dry-run never touches that branch but the helper is type-checked)

3_fan_out_axes.rationale: The captured outer stdout from `exec(cmd + " 2>&1; echo __RC=$?")` carries TWO orthogonal exit codes: (a) outer __RC=$? from shell echo — exit of the outer `hexa run dispatch ...` process, (b) inner __HEXA_RC=N — auto-appended by hexa.real exec() runtime, reporting rc of the inner shelled command. When dispatch shells through docker (hexa-runner:latest), docker exec can return non-zero while the outer wrapper still returns 0. Bench's per-record JSONL claimed status=fire on the outer signal alone — paint-over of an inner failure. Honest fix: parse BOTH; status=fail-inner-rc when outer=0 inner!=0.

Freshness smoke harness C1..C5 verified the 3-condition guard at commit 2d40c170. Inner-rc parser is a NEW concern operating BEFORE the freshness guard (status assignment changes from {fire,fail} to {fire,fail,fail-inner-rc}). C6 (fresh-witness + inner-rc-fail) and C7 (empty-witness + inner-rc-fail) cover the new branch without disturbing legacy cases. Correlation with rc-honesty axis < 0.7: rc-honesty is about the parse correctness (does hexa_rc=3 propagate?); regression-coverage is about non-disturbance of the prior guard semantics.

4_tier_1_promotion_gate_via_falsifier_pa.promotion_criterion: F1+F2+F3 PASS required (rc-honesty axis); F4+F5 PASS required (regression-coverage axis, no regression)
4_tier_1_promotion_gate_via_falsifier_pa.result: F1 PASS (logic trace + char-walker bounds); F2 PASS (parser added + smoke C6/C7); F3 PASS (smoke C6/C7 both fail-inner-rc/delta=0); F4 PASS (7/7 cases); F5 PASS (3 backends, witnesses=0, no errors)

root_cause.buggy_lines: tool/kick_bench.hexa:474-480 (pre-fix) — only outer __RC= parsed; let mut status = if rc == 0 { "fire" } else { "fail" }
root_cause.trigger_input: kick_dispatch.hexa shells `hexa run subagent_dispatch ...` which itself shells docker `hexa-runner:latest`. When the inner docker invocation fails (e.g. interp binary missing → rc=3 witness-not-captured), hexa.real's exec runtime appends __HEXA_RC=3 to the outer captured stdout. The shell wrapper `; echo __RC=$?` reports the exit of the OUTER hexa run command, which can still be 0 (or non-3) depending on how the failure propagated.
root_cause.why_misleading_fire: rc==0 (outer-ok) → status=fire; the inner __HEXA_RC=3 was unread. The freshness guard at commit 2d40c170 did NOT save the per-record because witness_path was empty (no witness emitted from a failed inner run), so the `else if status == "fire" && witness_path.len() > 0 && !fresh` branch never fired — status stayed "fire" with witness="" wall_time_ms=0. Outer counter remained honest (no increment because !fresh path); per-record JSONL emitted the misleading "fire".

fix_summary.primary_change: Added inner-rc parse in tool/kick_bench.hexa at L497-509 mirroring the outer __RC= parser pattern (last_index_of + offset + whitespace/underscore-bounded substring + to_int with try/catch). Status assignment at L537-543 now a 3-way ladder: outer rc!=0 → fail (worst); outer rc==0 + inner hexa_rc!=0 → fail-inner-rc (mid); both 0 → fire (best). Freshness guard branch at L547-552 unchanged in semantics but now naturally bypassed in the fail-inner-rc case because status!="fire".
fix_summary.secondary_change_outer_rc_parser: Hardened outer __RC= parser at L482-490 with explicit whitespace/underscore-bounded substring (was bare .substring(rc_idx+5, out.len()).trim()). Defensive against an adjacent __HEXA_RC= concatenation tail (e.g. `__RC=0__HEXA_RC=3` no-newline case observed in session_rollup_2026-04-27.md). F1 PASS preserved.

files_touched.change: L474-543 — outer __RC= parser hardened with bounded substring; new __HEXA_RC= parser block (L497-509); 3-way status ladder (L537-543) replacing 2-way conditional. Comments document self-found bug origin (ω-cycle live-spawn-smoke 2026-04-26).

_apply_guard signature extended with hexa_rc: i64 (L45); new 3-way status ladder mirrors kick_bench (L46-52); existing 5 callsites updated to pass hexa_rc=0 (preserves prior behavior); 2 NEW cases C6 (fresh+rc=0+hexa_rc=3 → fail-inner-rc/0) and C7 (empty+rc=0+hexa_rc=3 → fail-inner-rc/0) at L106-111.

tier_1_promotions.rationale: Per-record bench JSONL must reflect both outer and inner exit codes when the inner runtime is shelled (docker, ssh, hexa-runner, etc). Rule generalizes: any sentinel-decoder that captures stdout through hexa.real's exec() must consider __HEXA_RC= as the AUTHORITATIVE inner-rc, separate from any wrapper's own __RC=$?. Pattern reusable in tool/roadmap_integrate.hexa _exec_passthrough (already does it correctly), tool/kick_dispatch.hexa exec sites (multiple), and any future bench/orchestrator tool.
tier_1_promotions.evidence: tool/kick_bench.hexa:537-543 (3-way status ladder); tool/kick_bench_freshness_smoke.hexa:106-111 (C6/C7 PASS); pre-existing reference at tool/roadmap_integrate.hexa:267-298 (same pattern, same runtime contract).

falsifier_outcomes.evidence: logic trace at kick_bench.hexa:482-490 — char-walker breaks at whitespace/underscore so an adjacent __HEXA_RC= (no-newline concat) cannot bleed into the outer rc parse; try/catch fallthrough preserved; existing __RC=0 + newline case still extracts 0; existing __RC=2 case still extracts 2

kick_bench.hexa:497-509 — parser added; freshness smoke C6 (hexa_rc=3) yields status=fail-inner-rc proving the parser fed the status ladder; absence of __HEXA_RC= yields hexa_rc=0 default proving non-presence safe

tool/kick_bench_freshness_smoke.hexa C6 PASS (fresh witness + rc=0 + hexa_rc=3 → status=fail-inner-rc delta=0); C7 PASS (empty witness + rc=0 + hexa_rc=3 → status=fail-inner-rc delta=0); 7/7 sentinel __KICK_BENCH_FRESHNESS_SMOKE__ PASS cases=7 passes=7
hexa run tool/kick_bench_freshness_smoke.hexa observed sentinel `__KICK_BENCH_FRESHNESS_SMOKE__ PASS cases=7 passes=7` — 5 legacy + 2 new all green

hexa run tool/kick_bench.hexa bench-record-inner-rc-parse-fix-smoke --dry-run → 3 __KICK_BENCH_DRY__ records (claude_code, codex_cli, gemini_cli) + __KICK_BENCH__ PASS backends=3 witnesses=0; no parse error; new __HEXA_RC= block compiled cleanly

hook_dependency: NONE — no .git/hooks, no fswatch, no launchd added
raw_8_compliance: hexa-only honored — two .hexa edits (kick_bench.hexa, kick_bench_freshness_smoke.hexa); no new .py/.sh/.rs/.toml
atlas_protection: atlas.n6 untouched (L0 protection); shard lands at n6/atlas.append.bench-record-inner-rc-parse-fix.n6 via auto-ingest
honesty_preservation: no FAIL/NEAR/OBSTRUCTION/CONDITIONAL paint-over; the prior live-spawn-smoke OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED status remains unchanged. This fix addresses the bonus self-reported per-record JSONL bug from that ω-cycle only.
carry_forward_origin: ω-cycle live-spawn-smoke-with-minted-sid (2026-04-27) self-found T1-bench-wrapper-rc-parse-only-shallow-rc → carry-forward (a-bench-record) kick_bench.hexa parse __RC AND __HEXA_RC; per-record status="fail-inner-rc"
carry_forward_emit: (b-dispatch-rc-prop) tool/kick_dispatch.hexa exec sites (multiple) should be audited for the same outer/inner rc separation pattern. Specifically L929/L957 capture rc through `; echo __RC=$?` but do not parse __HEXA_RC=. If those sites ever feed a counter or status, a similar honesty patch will be needed.

(c-status-decoder) any post-hoc JSONL decoder should treat status="fail-inner-rc" identically to "fail" for aggregation purposes (do NOT count toward witnesses_landed); preserve the diagnostic distinction in dashboards.
